title: Narcissus integer
intro: The Narcissus integer, also known as the Autumn-flowering daffodil, is a perennial plant in the Amaryllidaceae family characterized by its striking, trumpet-shaped flowers and slender leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Narcissus integer include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or syrup to treat various health conditions, although its use should be cautious due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
taxonomy: Narcissus integer is classified in the plant kingdom as part of the Amaryllidaceae family, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the amaryllis family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its bulbous habit and showy flowers. Narcissus integer is further classified as a species within the genus Narcissus, which includes a wide range of daffodil species. The plant's classification is also supported by its morphological features, such as its trumpet-shaped flowers and strap-like leaves. As a member of the Amaryllidaceae family, Narcissus integer is closely related to other species within this family, including the common daffodil and the snowdrop.
uses: Narcissus integer is known for its potential medicinal uses, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Traditionally, the plant's bulb and flowers have been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments. Narcissus integer is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. Some studies suggest that the plant may also have antioxidant properties, which could help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. However, more research is necessary to fully understand the medicinal properties of Narcissus integer and its potential applications in human health.

constituents: Narcissus integer contains alkaloids, including homoplicine and tazettine, which contribute to its pharmacological properties. These compounds are responsible for the plant's potential medicinal applications.
